articles.title: Accuracy of machine learning in the diagnosis of odontogenic cysts and tumors: a systematic review and meta-analysis
articles.authors: Shrivastava PK; Hasan S; Abid L; et al.
articles.keywords: machine learning; odontogenic cysts; odontogenic tumors; OPG; CBCT; diagnosis
articles.abstract: Comprehensive literature search (Jan 2015–Dec 2022). Studies using ML for odontogenic cysts/tumors using OPG or CBCT included. 16 studies (10,872 cysts/tumors). Sensitivity/specificity for OPG: 0.83/0.82 (95% CI). CBCT studies: sensitivity=0.88, specificity=0.88 (95% CI 0.87–0.89). QUADAS-2 assessed risk of bias. Machine learning shows high diagnostic capability for odontogenic cysts and tumors via both imaging modalities.

articles.title: Dense image-mask attention-guided transformer network for jaw lesions classification and segmentation in dental CBCT images
articles.authors: Liu W; Li X; Liu C; Gao G; Xiong Y; Zhu T; et al.
articles.keywords: CBCT; jaw lesions; transformer; segmentation; classification; ameloblastoma; deep learning
articles.abstract: Automatic segmentation and classification of jaw lesions from CBCT images are crucial for computer-assisted diagnosis in oral and maxillofacial surgery. A dense image-mask attention-guided transformer network was proposed. A large in-house dataset of 358 CBCT scans with five types of jaw lesions was constructed. A task merge module employing per-lesion inference was designed. Results: binary segmentation DICE=90%, mean classification accuracy=89.23%, multi-class segmentation DICE=79.06%, surpassing many state-of-the-art methods.
